answers.Q1: Current parser supports stacked decorators with simple names. Limitation: no call expressions or attribute access in decorator position.
answers.Q2: Use Any/unknown fallback for decorator return-type inference in this iteration. Precise higher-order modeling deferred.
answers.Q3: @property, @staticmethod, @classmethod should be special-cased in the type checker with hardcoded semantics.
answers.Q4: Full PEP 614 — any expression in decorator position, including @buttons[0], @f()(x).

answers.Q1: Starred variable typed as list[T] where T is inferred from RHS element type, matching CPython Py3.12 behavior.
answers.Q2: Splat unpacking in function calls (*args, **kwargs) should also be addressed if not already supported — match Py3.12 behavior.
answers.Q3: Yes, include nested destructuring with stars (e.g., a, (*b, c) = ...) — full Py3.12 behavior.
answers.Q4: Use a runtime helper function: runtime::unpack_star(iter, n_before, n_after) for cleaner codegen.

answers.Q1: Check existing codegen for closures/captures and integrate nonlocal with whatever mechanism exists.
answers.Q2: Silently allowed, matching CPython behavior — defer to runtime NameError if accessed before assignment.
answers.Q3: Type checker should be updated to respect global/nonlocal annotations for correct type inference across scopes.
answers.Q4: nonlocal upgrades implicit read-only capture to mutable capture, matching CPython semantics.

answers.Q1: Check existing slice dispatch pattern and follow the same approach for step implementation.
answers.Q2: Check if 3-arg slice is already in AST/HIR — add parser/codegen changes as needed to pass step through.
answers.Q3: String step-slicing on Unicode codepoints, matching CPython behavior.
answers.Q4: a[::0] raises Python ValueError at runtime, matching CPython behavior.

answers.Q1: bytes as NaN-boxed heap object wrapping Vec<u8>, with ObjKind::Bytes in the existing value model.
answers.Q2: f-strings are already partially supported — not in scope for this change unless lexer prefix handling requires it.
answers.Q3: Full \N{name} Unicode named escapes — embed or query Unicode name database.
answers.Q4: Full CPython bytes API scope: find, replace, decode, startswith, endswith, split, join, hex, etc.
